name: RPG Narrative Writing Preset
description: Spec-Driven Development for interactive RPG narrative. Supports tabletop campaigns and computer RPGs across multiple rulesets (D&D 5e, Pathfinder 2e, Shadowrun 6e, generic), genres (Fantasy, Sci-Fi, Horror, Cyberpunk, Urban Fantasy, etc.), and export engines (Generic Markdown, Sugarcube, Ink, Yarn Spinner).
author: Andreas Daumann

rulesets.name: D&D 5e
rulesets.description: Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition with encounter scaling, CR balance, companion/faction mechanics

rulesets.name: Pathfinder 2e
rulesets.description: Pathfinder 2nd Edition mechanics and encounter design

rulesets.name: Shadowrun 6e
rulesets.description: Shadowrun 6th Edition with matrix, magic, and action economy

rulesets.name: Generic
rulesets.description: System-neutral templates for any RPG ruleset or custom systems

exportEngines.name: Generic
exportEngines.description: Annotated Markdown with hook blocks; engine-agnostic, portable

exportEngines.name: Sugarcube
exportEngines.description: Twee 3 / Sugarcube 2 macros for browser-based interactive fiction

exportEngines.name: Ink
exportEngines.description: Inkle's dialogue scripting language for narrative-heavy games

exportEngines.name: Yarn Spinner
exportEngines.description: Yarn Spinner dialogue system for Unity and Godot RPGs

features: Flexible ruleset selection (D&D 5e complete, others planned)
Platform targeting (Tabletop vs Computer Game with engine filtering)
Ruleset-specific templates and mechanics when available
Multiple export engines (Generic, Sugarcube, Ink, Yarn Spinner)
Dynamic NPC relationships and dialogue trees
Multiple ending paths with lock timing
